4	from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks

8	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
9	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
10	        prog="bookmark",
11	        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
12	    )
13	    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")
14	
15	    # --- add ---
16	    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
17	    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
18	    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
19	    add_parser.add_argument(
20	        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
21	    )
22	    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")
23	
24	    # --- list ---
25	    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
26	    list_parser.add_argument(
27	        "--format",
28	        choices=["table", "json"],
29	        default="table",
30	        help="Output format (default: table)",
31	    )
32	
33	    # --- delete ---
34	    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
35	    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")
36	
37	    # --- show ---
38	    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
39	    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")
40	
41	    return parser
42	
43	
44	def main(argv=None):
45	    parser = build_parser()
46	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
47	
48	    if args.command is None:
49	        parser.print_help()
50	        sys.exit(1)
51	
52	    if args.command == "add":
53	        bookmark = add_bookmark(
54	            url=args.url,
55	            title=args.title,
56	            tags=args.tags,
57	            notes=args.notes,
58	        )
59	        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")
60	
61	    elif args.command == "list":
62	        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
63	        if args.format == "json":
64	            print(format_json(bookmarks))
65	        else:
66	            print(format_table(bookmarks))
67	
68	    elif args.command == "delete":
69	        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
70	        if success:
71	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
72	        else:
73	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
74	            sys.exit(1)
75	
76	    elif args.command == "show":
77	        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
78	        if bookmark is None:
79	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
80	            sys.exit(1)
81	        print(format_table([bookmark]))
82	
83	
84	if __name__ == "__main__":
85	    main()

1	import json
2	import os
3	import uuid
4	from typing import List, Optional
5	
6	from .models import Bookmark
7	
8	DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")
9	
10	
11	def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
12	    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
13	    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
14	        return []
15	    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
16	        data = json.load(f)
17	    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]
18	
19	
20	def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
21	    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
22	    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
23	    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
24	        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)
25	
26	
27	def add_bookmark(
28	    url: str,
29	    title: str,
30	    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
31	    notes: str = "",
32	    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
33	) -> Bookmark:
34	    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
35	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
36	    bookmark = Bookmark(
37	        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
38	        url=url,
39	        title=title,
40	        tags=tags or [],
41	        notes=notes,
42	    )
43	    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
44	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
45	    return bookmark
46	
47	
48	def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
49	    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
50	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
51	    original_len = len(bookmarks)
52	    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
53	    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
54	        return False
55	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
56	    return True
57	
58	
59	def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
60	    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
61	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
62	    for b in bookmarks:
63	        if b.id == bookmark_id:
64	            return b
65	    return None

1	import json
2	from typing import List
3	
4	from .models import Bookmark
5	
6	
7	def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
8	    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
9	    if not bookmarks:
10	        return "No bookmarks found."
11	
12	    # Column widths
13	    id_w = 8
14	    title_w = 30
15	    url_w = 40
16	    tags_w = 20
17	
18	    header = (
19	        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
20	        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
21	        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
22	        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
23	    )
24	    separator = "-" * len(header)
25	    lines = [header, separator]
26	
27	    for b in bookmarks:
28	        short_id = b.id[:8]
29	        title = b.title[:title_w]
30	        url = b.url[:url_w]
31	        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
32	        lines.append(
33	            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
34	            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
35	            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
36	            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
37	        )
38	
39	    return "\n".join(lines)
40	
41	
42	def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
43	    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
44	    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import json
from typing import List

from .models import Bookmark


def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
    if not bookmarks:
        return "No bookmarks found."

    # Column widths
    id_w = 8
    title_w = 30
    url_w = 40
    tags_w = 20

    header = (
        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
    )
    separator = "-" * len(header)
    lines = [header, separator]

    for b in bookmarks:
        short_id = b.id[:8]
        title = b.title[:title_w]
        url = b.url[:url_w]
        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
        lines.append(
            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
        )

    return "\n".join(lines)


def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

# Plan: Add Search Functionality to Bookmark CLI

## Context

The bookmark CLI (`src/cli.py`) currently supports `add`, `list`, `delete`, and `show` commands but has no way to filter or find bookmarks. This plan adds a `search` command backed by a new `src/search.py` module. The test file `tests/test_search.py` already exists and defines the full specification — all tests must pass.

---

## Critical Files

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `src/search.py` | **Create** — new module with 4 search functions |
| `src/cli.py` | **Edit** — add `search` subparser + handler + import |
| `tests/test_search.py` | Read-only reference (must pass) |

Reuse from existing codebase:
- `format_table` / `format_json` from `src/formatter.py` — already used by `list` and `show`
- `load_bookmarks` from `src/storage.py` — already used by `list`
- `Bookmark` dataclass from `src/models.py`

---

## Step-by-Step Implementation

### Step 1 — Create `src/search.py`

New file. Use only stdlib (`urllib.parse`).

```python
from typing import List, Optional
from urllib.parse import urlparse

from .models import Bookmark


def search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Case-insensitive substring match on title."""
    q = query.lower()
    return [b for b in bookmarks if q in b.title.lower()]


def search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Exact match against each bookmark's tags list."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if tag in b.tags]


def search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Exact match against the URL's netloc (e.g. 'github.com', 'docs.python.org')."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if urlparse(b.url).netloc == domain]


def search(
    bookmarks: List[Bookmark],
    title: Optional[str] = None,
    tag: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Combine criteria with AND logic. No criteria → return all bookmarks."""
```

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import json
import os
import uuid
from typing import List, Optional

from .models import Bookmark

DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")


def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
        return []
    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
        data = json.load(f)
    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]


def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)


def add_bookmark(
    url: str,
    title: str,
    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    notes: str = "",
    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
) -> Bookmark:
    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    bookmark = Bookmark(
        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
        url=url,
        title=title,
        tags=tags or [],
        notes=notes,
    )
    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
    return bookmark


def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    original_len = len(bookmarks)
    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
        return False
    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
    return True


def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    for b in bookmarks:
        if b.id == bookmark_id:
            return b
    return None

import json
from typing import List

from .models import Bookmark


def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
    if not bookmarks:
        return "No bookmarks found."

    # Column widths
    id_w = 8
    title_w = 30
    url_w = 40
    tags_w = 20

    header = (
        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
    )
    separator = "-" * len(header)
    lines = [header, separator]

    for b in bookmarks:
        short_id = b.id[:8]
        title = b.title[:title_w]
        url = b.url[:url_w]
        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
        lines.append(
            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
        )

    return "\n".join(lines)


def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

import pytest

from src.models import Bookmark

    add_bookmark,

    get_bookmark,
    load_bookmarks,
    save_bookmarks,
)


@pytest.fixture
def db_path(tmp_path):
    return str(tmp_path / "test_bookmarks.json")


@pytest.fixture
def sample_bookmarks():
    return [
        Bookmark(
            id="aaa-111",
            url="https://example.com",
            title="Example Site",
            tags=["example", "test"],
            notes="A sample bookmark",
            created_at="2026-01-01T00:00:00",
        ),
        Bookmark(
            id="bbb-222",
            url="https://docs.python.org/3/",
            title="Python Docs",
            tags=["python", "docs"],
            notes="Official Python documentation",
            created_at="2026-01-02T00:00:00",
        ),
    ]


class TestLoadBookmarks:
    def test_returns_empty_list_when_file_missing(self, db_path):
        result = load_bookmarks(db_path)
        assert result == []

    def test_loads_bookmarks_from_file(self, db_path, sample_bookmarks):
        save_bookmarks(sample_bookmarks, db_path)
        result = load_bookmarks(db_path)
        assert len(result) == 2
        assert result[0].id == "aaa-111"
        assert result[1].title == "Python Docs"


class TestSaveBookmarks:
    def test_creates_file(self, db_path, sample_bookmarks):
        save_bookmarks(sample_bookmarks, db_path)
        assert os.path.exists(db_path)

    def test_file_contains_valid_json(self, db_path, sample_bookmarks):
        save_bookmarks(sample_bookmarks, db_path)
        with open(db_path) as f:
            data = json.load(f)
        assert len(data) == 2
        assert data[0]["url"] == "https://example.com"


class TestAddBookmark:
    def test_adds_bookmark_and_returns_it(self, db_path):
        result = add_bookmark(
            url="https://new.example.com",
            title="New Bookmark",
            tags=["new"],
            db_path=db_path,
        )
        assert result.url == "https://new.example.com"
        assert result.title == "New Bookmark"

    def test_persists_bookmark(self, db_path):
        add_bookmark(url="https://a.com", title="A", db_path=db_path)
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
        assert len(bookmarks) == 1

    def test_generates_unique_ids(self, db_path):
        b1 = add_bookmark(url="https://a.com", title="A", db_path=db_path)
        b2 = add_bookmark(url="https://b.com", title="B", db_path=db_path)
        assert b1.id != b2.id


class TestDeleteBookmark:
    def test_deletes_existing_bookmark(self, db_path, sample_bookmarks):
        save_bookmarks(sample_bookmarks, db_path)
        result = delete_bookmark("aaa-111", db_path)
        assert result is True
        remaining = load_bookmarks(db_path)
        assert len(remaining) == 1

    def test_returns_false_for_missing_id(self, db_path, sample_bookmarks):
        save_bookmarks(sample_bookmarks, db_path)
        result = delete_bookmark("nonexistent", db_path)
        assert result is False


class TestGetBookmark:
    def test_returns_bookmark_by_id(self, db_path, sample_bookmarks):
        save_bookmarks(sample_bookmarks, db_path)
        result = get_bookmark("bbb-222", db_path)
        assert result is not None
        assert result.title == "Python Docs"

    def test_returns_none_for_missing_id(self, db_path, sample_bookmarks):
        save_bookmarks(sample_bookmarks, db_path)
        result = get_bookmark("nonexistent", db_path)
        assert result is None

from typing import List, Optional
from urllib.parse import urlparse

from .models import Bookmark


def search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks whose title contains query (case-insensitive substring match)."""
    q = query.lower()
    return [b for b in bookmarks if q in b.title.lower()]


def search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks that have an exact match for the given tag."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if tag in b.tags]


def search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks whose URL netloc matches the given domain exactly."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if urlparse(b.url).netloc == domain]


def search(
    bookmarks: List[Bookmark],
    title: Optional[str] = None,
    tag: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Search bookmarks with AND logic across all provided criteria.

    If no criteria are given, all bookmarks are returned.
    """
    results = bookmarks
    if title is not None:
        results = search_by_title(results, title)
    if tag is not None:
        results = search_by_tag(results, tag)
    if domain is not None:
        results = search_by_domain(results, domain)
    return results

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    # --- search ---
    search_parser = subparsers.add_parser("search", help="Search bookmarks")
    search_parser.add_argument("--title", default=None, help="Filter by title (case-insensitive substring)")
    search_parser.add_argument("--tag", default=None, help="Filter by tag (exact match)")
    search_parser.add_argument("--domain", default=None, help="Filter by URL domain (exact match)")
    search_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
